engine.ladder_reasoning: Sells a defined professional expertise (21-yr designer, MDIA/KBDI credentials, priced consultation services); the visitor is deciding to trust a person/firm, so credentials + testimonials + process carry the sale, not the portfolio alone.

goal.one_action: Book (or call for) an initial paid design/colour consultation

structure.archetype_note: Classic multi-page consultant brochure: home → about (founder bio+credentials) → services (priced) → testimonials → portfolio (category galleries) → contact/booking. Calendly is the booking handoff; a site-wide newsletter form is the recurring secondary capture.

mapped.observed_as: Professional Interior Designer Creating Harmonious Environments
Our Services (priced service list: Interior Design, Colour Consulting, Bathroom/Kitchen Design, Sourcing, Window Coverings)
Testimonials (4 named client quotes with suburbs)
We'd love to work with you / Ready to get started?

unmapped.observed: Portfolio organized as 10 room/category galleries each opening a project gallery
unmapped.why_no_fit: sectionList has no dedicated portfolio/gallery section id; mapped to miscellaneous

confidence.why: fetch_quality=full; 6 of 9 pages crawled with clear sections, CTAs, priced services, credentials and testimonials; only FAQs/Blog uncrawled, which don't change the engine or goal.

gap_findings: Primary CTA 'Call or Book Now' is dual (tel + Calendly booking) plus a site-wide newsletter form — the single-mechanism goal model doesn't cleanly express a page whose conversion splits across call, external booking, and list-capture.
Portfolio-as-category-galleries has no first-class section id (forced into miscellaneous).
